changedFiles.reason: Generated active Work Item status through the lifecycle command.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Lifecycle commands must leave generated status consistent before returning success.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: ai-start generates active status; failure restores the previous status and removes the new pair.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Coverage defaults must cover advertised common layouts without treating known test layouts as production.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Matrix tests cover Android, Swift, C#, Java, and Dart production plus Android, Swift, and C# test layouts.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Installer detection must parse effective Makefile lines rather than comments.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Anchored line matching excludes commented includes; regression test verifies an active include is appended.

guidelinesCompliance.guideline: Do not hand-edit generated cockpit status.
guidelinesCompliance.evidence: Status changes were produced through generate-cockpit-status and ai-start.

risk.detail: Reported installation, lifecycle, and common stack coverage defects are covered by unit and temporary-repository smoke tests.

observedIssues: Coverage policy glob semantics treat patterns ending in /** as literal directory prefixes; explicit common roots were used.

residualRisks.detail: Unconventional or nested project layouts still require repository-specific production and test patterns.
The Japanese landing page is improved but the complete documentation set was not rewritten.

reviewReadiness.reason: All reported functional defects are fixed and tested; project-specific guard configuration remains an explicit adoption responsibility.

userCorrectionsCaptured: Do not create a new branch.
userCorrectionSolidification: Work remained on the existing main branch.

checkpointEvidence.detail: Installation, lifecycle, coverage, and documentation scope reviewed.
Full tests, temporary installation lifecycle smoke test, documentation, and residual risks reviewed.

knownGaps: Coverage Guard does not infer arbitrary repository layouts.
No reduced Contract schema or interactive initializer was introduced.
overclaimPrevention: Support claims distinguish the language-agnostic governance runtime from customizable quality and guard presets.
